latest.title: Appointment of new Non-Executive Director
latest.content:
25 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 6515J
ITM Power PLC
25 June 2026
25 June 2026
ITM Power plc
Change of Linde Board Representative
ITM Power is pleased to announce that Oleg Williamson has been appointed a Non-Executive Director effective from 24 June 2026. He replaces Matthias von Plotho, who has resigned from the Board on the same day. Matthias has served as Linde's nominated Board representative since January 2025.
Oleg joined Linde in 2022 as Executive Director and Assistant Treasurer. Previous to Linde, Oleg has held senior corporate treasury positions at several large industrial multinationals across three continents.
Jürgen Nowicki, Chairman of ITM, said: "I would like to thank Matthias for his contribution over the last 18 months, and am delighted to welcome Oleg to the board. The Board looks forward to working with him and bringing his strong corporate finance and treasury background to support ITM's future growth ambitions."
Disclosures required by Schedule Two, paragraph (g) of the AIM Rules for Companies
Oleg Charles Y Williamson, who is 45 years old, is or has been a director of the following companies during the past five years:
Current Directorships / Partnerships
Former Directorships / Partnerships (within the last 5 years)
Praxair International Finance UC
Acadia International Insurance dac
Kosmoid Finance UC
Linde (formerly: Rounderway Limited)
As at the date of this announcement, Oleg Williamson does not hold any ordinary shares in the capital of the Company.
There are no further disclosures required pursuant to Schedule Two, paragraph (g) of the AIM Rules for Companies in respect of Oleg Williamson's appointment to the Board.
For further information, please visit
www.itm-power.com
or contact:
ITM Power plc
Justin Scarborough, Head of Investor Relations
Huan Quayle, General Counsel & Company Secretary
+44 (0)114 551 1080
Berenberg
Ciaran Walsh, Harry Nicholas
+44 (0)20 3207 7800
J.P. Morgan Cazenove
Richard Perelman, Charles Oakes
+44 (0)20 7742 4000
About ITM Power plc:
ITM Power designs and manufactures industrial-scale electrolysers for green hydrogen production. Its proprietary PEM technology, vertically integrated manufacturing, and in-house engineering capabilities enable it to supply standardised, high-performance hydrogen plants.
With a proven track record in large-scale projects, ITM Power helps leading industrial and energy companies achieve their decarbonisation goals. In addition to its equipment offering, the company provides reliable and cost-competitive hydrogen supply through its build, own and operate (BOO) model, Hydropulse.
Headquartered in the UK, ITM Power is listed on the London Stock Exchange AIM (LSE: ITM) and holds the Green Economy Mark, which recognises companies that generate over 50% of their revenues from green products and services.

latest.title: ITM and DB Systemtechnik GmbH LOI
latest.content:
24 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 4552J
ITM Power PLC
24 June 2026
24 June 2026
ITM Power plc
ITM and DB Systemtechnik to forge an innovation and research partnership,starting with a joint FEED study
ITM Power plc and DB Systemtechnik GmbH have signed a Letter of Intent (LOI) to forge an innovation and research partnership for the development, piloting and testing of green energy solutions for the transport and critical infrastructure sectors.
This builds on the agreement signed between ITM Power and Deutsche Bahn in March 2025 to collaborate on sustainable transportation and infrastructure solutions. The collaboration will explore opportunities to deploy green hydrogen to support the decarbonisation of rail transport and associated mobility solutions, including locomotive, bus, and truck fleets, as well as the energy resilience requirements of railway infrastructure. The partnership will also evaluate solutions that enhance energy resilience and self-sufficiency, including the production, sourcing and storage of green fuels for day-to-day operations.
As an initial step, ITM Power and DB Systemtechnik intend to undertake a Front-End Engineering Design (FEED) study to assess the technical configuration, site integration, operational requirements and commercial framework for the potential applications of ITM's electrolyser technology at Deutsche Bahn sites.
Dennis Schulz, CEO of ITM Power, said: "We are delighted to take the next step in our close collaboration. The agreement and planned FEED study mark an important progression in our relationship and further underline the potential role of green hydrogen in the decarbonisation of critical transport infrastructure."
Dr. Hiie-Mai Unger, CEO of DB Systemtechnik, said: "The collaboration with ITM Power underlines our commitment to driving innovation in sustainable railway systems. The planned FEED study will provide valuable insights into how green hydrogen can be integrated into our infrastructure, strengthening both decarbonisation efforts and operational resilience across the DB network."
For further information, please visit
www.itm-power.com
or contact:
ITM Power plc
Justin Scarborough, Head of Investor Relations
+44 (0)114 551 1080
Berenberg
Ciaran Walsh, Harry Nicholas
+44 (0)20 3207 7800
J.P. Morgan Cazenove
Richard Perelman, Charles Oakes
+44 (0)20 7742 4000
About ITM Power plc:
ITM Power designs and manufactures industrial-scale electrolysers for green hydrogen production. Its proprietary PEM technology, vertically integrated manufacturing, and in-house engineering capabilities enable it to supply standardised, high-performance hydrogen plants.
With a proven track record in large-scale projects, ITM Power helps leading industrial and energy companies achieve their decarbonisation goals. In addition to its equipment offering, the company provides reliable and cost-competitive hydrogen supply through its build, own and operate (BOO) model, Hydropulse.
Headquartered in the UK, ITM Power is listed on the London Stock Exchange AIM (LSE: ITM) and holds the Green Economy Mark, which recognises companies that generate over 50% of their revenues from green products and services.
About DB Systemtechnik GmbH:
DB Systemtechnik is Europe's leading provider of engineering and testing services in the railway sector and is committed to innovation across the technical spectrum of the railway system. In this context, it also focuses on innovative solutions for energy supply. DB Systemtechnik is a member of the Deutsche Bahn group.

latest.title: Strategic partnership with Protium
latest.content:
3 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 7199G
ITM Power PLC
03 June 2026
3 June 2026
ITM Power plc
Strategic Partnership with Protium for UK green hydrogen projects
Protium Green Solutions Ltd ("Protium") and ITM Power have formalised a strategic partnership to develop, invest in, and operate industrial-scale green hydrogen production plants across the UK, with an immediate focus on advancing the Cromarty Project in Scotland.
The collaboration establishes a flexible framework to support the expansion of the domestic green energy supply chain within the UK economy. Under the new agreement, the companies are collectively exploring a range of deployment models for future UK projects, with a particular focus on Protium's portfolio of Hydrogen Allocation Round (HAR) projects.
The companies are evaluating multiple avenues for collaboration, potentially including via Hydropulse, ITM Power's build-own-operate subsidiary designed to deliver containerised green hydrogen plants, as well as options for Protium to directly acquire ITM's market-leading electrolysis assets. This parallel tracking allows both parties to undertake rigorous commercial due diligence to ensure optimal project delivery, technical alignment, and value for money.
The first area of collaboration being actively explored under this framework is the flagship Cromarty Hydrogen Project in the Scottish Highlands. Recently acquired by Protium, the project successfully secured government-backed funding through Hydrogen Allocation Round 1 (HAR1).
Cromarty, with 15 MW electrolyser capacity, will produce circa 7 tonnes of green hydrogen per day at peak output. The project will provide a localised, reliable energy source to decarbonise regional sectors, targeting a mixture of heavy industrial heat and power customers, many of whom are currently off the national gas grid. Phase 1 of the Cromarty project alone is estimated to create approximately 30 highly skilled local jobs and apprenticeships within the region.
As the primary project developer, Protium will lead power procurement, permitting, downstream infrastructure, and hydrogen distribution. A Final Investment Decision (FID) for the Cromarty project is currently targeted for December 2026.
Dennis Schulz, CEO of ITM Power, said: "By working alongside Protium in a framework ranging from equipment supply to investment, we are bringing together our complementary expertise. Together, we can deliver highly competitive hydrogen with strong long-term economics and help accelerate the UK hydrogen market."
Christopher Jackson, CEO and Founder of Protium, said: "This partnership between Protium and ITM Power brings two of the UK's best-in-class hydrogen businesses together to deliver a truly unbeatable proposition for customers. Reliability, affordability and scalability with no compromises and no carbon emissions. This is how we move the UK hydrogen economy from concept to reality."
For further information, please visit
www.itm-power.com
or contact:
ITM Power plc
Justin Scarborough, Head of Investor Relations
+44 (0)114 551 1080
Berenberg
Ciaran Walsh, Harry Nicholas
+44 (0)20 3207 7800
J.P. Morgan Cazenove
Richard Perelman, Charles Oakes
+44 (0)20 7742 4000
About ITM Power plc:
ITM Power designs and manufactures industrial-scale electrolysers for green hydrogen production. Its proprietary PEM technology, vertically integrated manufacturing, and in-house engineering capabilities enable it to supply standardised, high-performance hydrogen plants.
With a proven track record in large-scale projects, ITM Power helps leading industrial and energy companies achieve their decarbonisation goals. In addition to its equipment offering, the company provides reliable and cost-competitive hydrogen supply through its build, own and operate (BOO) model, Hydropulse.
Headquartered in the UK, ITM Power is listed on the London Stock Exchange AIM (LSE: ITM) and holds the Green Economy Mark, which recognises companies that generate over 50% of their revenues from green products and services.
About Protium:
Protium is a green hydrogen company. Its end-to-end solutions help businesses decarbonise reliably, affordably and safely by replacing fossil fuels across construction, manufacturing, transport and operations.
By pioneering the scale-up and adoption of green hydrogen and its derivatives in the UK, Protium delivers practical hydrogen solutions that work today, spanning production, distribution and on-site deployment, supported by expert guidance from technical services to commercial development, to enable a confident transition to a cleaner, more resilient energy future.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: ITM Ltd. supplies specialised industrial components, mainly precision‑engineered connectors and housings used in aerospace and energy systems. Its products are sold to OEMs and system integrators that require high reliability.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits at the intersection of two growing markets: the shift to electrified powertrains in the automotive sector and the expansion of offshore renewable energy. Its parts are essential for high‑temperature, high‑pressure environments, giving it a niche advantage over generic suppliers.
company_30s.current_state: ITM is still in a fragile growth phase, operating with limited cash flow and a thin order book. Recent filings show it is actively seeking new capital and partnerships to stay afloat. The share price is trading at a very low level, well below what investors would expect for a firm with potential in these sectors.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy reshaping its board and securing strategic alliances. A new non‑executive director was appointed and a letter of intent was signed with DB Systemtechnik GmbH, hinting at a joint venture or supply agreement.

ITM has also announced a partnership with Protium, a firm known for hydrogen‑fuel cell technology, and a collaboration with Rheinmetall, a major defence contractor. These moves suggest the company is positioning itself within high‑value, high‑barrier markets.

Capital‑raising activity is evident from the 40 m investment from GB Energy and the 46.5 m grant from DESNZ. Share‑based incentives for the CEO and a block‑listing application further indicate management is trying to shore up shareholder confidence.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent speculation – traders are watching but not yet betting.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some traders see the new partnerships as a doorway into lucrative aerospace and hydrogen markets, arguing that a successful collaboration could lift the company’s valuation.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others worry that the company’s thin earnings base and high debt exposure could lead to a liquidity crunch if the partnerships don’t materialise quickly.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has moved from cautious observation to a more optimistic tone after the Protium deal announcement, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for ITM is flat – there is no current public interest, which is a good sign for a contrarian play.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the Protium partnership – a signed agreement or a joint product launch would be a clear positive signal.
Finalisation of the LOI with DB Systemtechnik – if the deal moves beyond intent to a signed contract, it could inject capital and orders.
Announcement of the block‑listing application outcome – a successful listing could unlock liquidity and attract institutional buyers.
Any grant disbursement from DESNZ or GB Energy – the cash flow from these grants could be used for R&D or debt repayment.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike on the daily chart, a breakout above the recent high, or a clear reversal pattern around the 99p support level. A sudden increase in institutional buying or a positive analyst upgrade would also confirm the thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early phase of a potential recovery. The APEX compression score indicates a tight range, suggesting a spring is ready to snap. If the company can move past the 99p level, the next leg could be a rapid climb.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company delivers on its partnership agreements, the demand for its specialised components could surge. A rebound from the current trough to even 20p would represent a 2000% return, while a move to 30p would be a 3000% gain – well beyond typical market swings.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest risk is that the announced deals fall through or that the company cannot meet its cash needs. In that scenario, the price could slide further into the low single‑digit pence, wiping out any gains from a brief bounce.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is huge relative to the small downside risk of a further 10‑15p drop. The asymmetry is favourable: a small loss could be offset by a large, rapid gain if the catalysts play out.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a break above 99p with a stop 5p below that level. Size the position to keep maximum exposure at 1% of available capital, given the low liquidity. Use a trailing stop of 10p to protect gains.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until you see a volume spike of at least 50% above the 20‑day average or a clear bullish reversal pattern. Also wait for the Protium or DB Systemtechnik deal to be formally announced.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – no immediate signs of delisting or bankruptcy.

ai_insights.bottom_line: ITM Ltd. sits on the edge of a potential rebound triggered by fresh strategic partnerships; if those deals materialise, the upside is large compared to a modest downside risk.
